name: Naga

visual_attributes.size_and_scale: varies from human size to large serpent
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: serpent body with human torso and head

domain_specific_visual.iconography: often depicted with jewels and treasures
domain_specific_visual.environment: associated with water bodies and underground realms

functional_attributes.primary_function: guardian of treasures and water bodies

domain_specific_functional.mythological_role: semi-divine beings with powers over water and treasures
domain_specific_functional.cultural_role: worshipped in rituals and folk traditions

relationships.description: Nāgas are the children of the sage Kashyapa and Kadru.
Garuḍa is the eternal archrival of the Nāgas.
Nagaraja is the king of the Nāgas.

contextual_attributes.origin: South Asian and Southeast Asian mythologies
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Nāgas hold cultural significance in Hinduism and Buddhism as divine or semi-divine beings.

contrastive_constraints: NOT devoid of cultural and mythological context
